diagrams.title: Distributed System Architecture
diagrams.description: A conceptual diagram showing multiple nodes (computers) connected through a communication network, with users seeing a single unified interface. The diagram illustrates how individual independent computers appear as one coherent system to end users.
elements: Multiple Nodes/Sites (labeled Node 1, Node 2, Node 3, ..., Node N)
Message Passing Arrows (bidirectional between nodes and network)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how distributed systems are structured and how transparency is achieved through the network layer hiding the distribution from users.

diagrams.title: Transparency Layers in Distributed Systems
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing the six types of transparency arranged from user-visible to system-level. Users interact with the top layer while the system handles lower layers transparently.
elements: Top Layer: User View (single system appearance)
Bottom Layer: Actual Distributed Infrastructure (network, heterogeneous nodes)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate the different transparency types that collectively make a distributed system appear as a single coherent system to users.

diagrams.title: Centralized vs Distributed System
diagrams.description: A comparison diagram showing a centralized system (single central computer with multiple terminals) versus a distributed system (multiple independent computers connected via network, appearing as one).
elements: Left side: Centralized System (one powerful computer, multiple thin clients/terminals)
Right side: Distributed System (multiple equal nodes connected by network, users see single system)
Communication paths shown differently for each architecture
Scalability indicators (vertical arrow for centralized, horizontal for distributed)
diagrams.purpose: Clearly differentiate between centralized and distributed architectures, highlighting how distribution changes system characteristics and trade-offs.
